content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All five amendments identify real, verified defects in the planned-elsewhere reconciliation subsystem. Two amendments (fr-3-1efa3b60 and fr-3-d9102962) describe the same underlying bug (verification state never persisted) from different angles—both are valid findings. The remaining three identify distinct issues: a force-completion logic gap, missing sync durability for comments, and a missing journal-rebuild fallback contradicting the documented design.

## Votes

- `fr-3-1efa3b60`: Accept
  Verified: verify_and_comment_mappings (planned_elsewhere.rs:247-324) returns MappingVerificationOutcome objects but no caller persists verified=true back to the NDJSON sidecar. success_reconciliation.rs filters on !mapped_bead_verified (line 699-702) to select unverified entries, so every subsequent reconciliation run will re-verify the same mappings and re-post duplicate br comments. This is a concrete data integrity and idempotency bug. The proposed fix (update NDJSON entries or adopt append-only verification events) is sound.
- `fr-3-3bd1a49d`: Accept
  Verified: The force-completed check at final_review.rs:1503 uses `!final_accepted_amendments.is_empty() && final_review_restart_count >= max_restarts` without filtering out planned-elsewhere amendments. The code at line 1551-1553 correctly distinguishes via `any(|a| a.mapped_to_bead_id.is_none())` for the restart decision, but the early return at 1503 lacks this filter. When all accepted amendments are planned-elsewhere (mapped_to_bead_id is Some), the early return fires unnecessarily, setting force_completed=true and emitting a misleading summary. The proposed fix to add the same mapped_to_bead_id check is correct and consistent with the existing logic downstream.
- `fr-3-46f7f076`: Accept
  Verified: comment_bead() in br_process.rs calls exec_tracked_mutation which sets has_unsync_mutations=true, but planned_elsewhere.rs never calls sync_flush() after posting comments. success_reconciliation.rs:200-209 does call sync after bead close mutations, but the planned-elsewhere comment path has no such guarantee. If the process exits between comment posting and an eventual sync, comments are lost. The fix (calling sync_flush after comments or batching before a final sync) directly addresses this data loss risk.
- `fr-3-d9102962`: Accept
  Verified: This identifies the same core defect as fr-3-1efa3b60 (verification never persisted) but frames it from the engine/journal perspective—engine.rs:7618 records mapped_bead_verified:false, and no code path ever updates this to true in durable storage. The amendment correctly notes that the reconciliation filter (success_reconciliation.rs:699-718) will perpetually re-select the same mappings. While overlapping with fr-3-1efa3b60, both are valid findings and the journal-persistence angle adds value since the journal is the authoritative audit record.
- `fr-3-a1a9ae8d`: Accept
  Verified: record_planned_elsewhere_mapping (service.rs:2348-2390) documents that 'the NDJSON file can be rebuilt from the journal', but load_planned_elsewhere_mappings (service.rs:2395-2401) only reads the sidecar via mapping_store.read_mappings() with NO journal fallback—returning an empty vec if the sidecar is missing. The engine (engine.rs:7643-7656) logs and continues on sidecar-write failure. This means a crash after journal append but before sidecar write leaves a mapping permanently invisible to reload, contradicting the documented recovery guarantee. The fix (implementing journal-rebuild fallback or atomic writes) closes a real durability gap.
